{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in the documents, the wrist joint is formed by the articulations between the distal radius and the scaphoid, lunate, and triquetrum. The ulna is not part of the wrist joint itself, as it articulates with the distal via the distal radioulnar joint. Therefore, the ulna is the bone that is not involved in the formation of the wrist joint.", "answer_choice": "B"}
